input.prompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,

input.initialPrompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

data.content:
Here's the standard approach:

## 1. Define Resource Dictionaries per theme

Create separate XAML resource dictionaries:

```xml
<!-- Themes/LightTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
                    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#1A1A1A</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#0078D4</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

Repeat for `DarkTheme.xaml` and `HighContrastTheme.xaml` using the same keys with different values.

## 2. Merge a default theme in App.xaml

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <ResourceDictionary>
        <ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
            <ResourceDictionary Source="Themes/LightTheme.xaml" />
        </ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
    </ResourceDictionary>
</Application.Resources>
```

## 3. Create a theme-switching service

```csharp
public static class ThemeService
{
    public static void ApplyTheme(string themeName)
    {
        var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
        mergedDicts.Clear();

        var theme = themeName switch
        {
            "Dark" => new DarkTheme(),
            "HighContrast" => new HighContrastTheme(),
            _ => new LightTheme()
        };

        mergedDicts.Add(theme);

        // Persist choice
        Preferences.Set("AppTheme", themeName);
    }
}
```

## 4. Use `DynamicResource` in your UI

This is critical — `StaticResource` won't update at runtime:

```xml
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource PageBackground}">
    <Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" Text="Hello" />
</ContentPage>
```

## 5. Wire up the settings page

```xml
<Picker x:Name="ThemePicker" SelectedIndexChanged="OnThemeChanged">
    <Picker.Items>
        <x:String>Light</x:String>
        <x:String>Dark</x:String>
        <x:String>HighContrast</x:String>
    </Picker.Items>
</Picker>
```

```csharp
void OnThemeChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    var selected = ThemePicker.Items[ThemePicker.SelectedIndex];
    ThemeService.ApplyTheme(selected);
}
```

## 6. Restore on startup (in App.xaml.cs)

```csharp
public App()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    var saved = Preferences.Get("AppTheme", "Light");
    ThemeService.ApplyTheme(saved);
}
```

**Key points:**
- Always use `DynamicResource` so bindings update live when dictionaries swap.
- Keep the same `x:Key` names across all three dictionaries.
- Use `Preferences` (or `SecureStorage`) to persist the user's choice.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking how to set up themed styling in a .NET MAUI app with three branded themes (light, dark, high-contrast) that can be switched at runtime from a settings page. This is a general "how-to" question, not a code task in a specific repository. I'll provide a concise architectural answer.
